items.title: AMD announces MI350P PCIe AI accelerator card with 144GB of HBM3E — roughly 40% faster in FP16 and FP8 theoretical compute compared to Nvidia's H200 NVL competitor
items.description: AMD launches a new MI350P PCIe AI-accelerator card with half the cores and memory of its flagship Instinct MI355X GPU. The new card provides customers with a drop-in upgrade solution for existing air-cooled servers.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD has launched a new member of the MI350-series that comes in a PCIe form factor. The new Instinct MI350P comes with 128 CUs and 144GB of HBM3E memory and is designed to be a drop-in upgrade solution for existing air-cooled servers.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingThe MI350P comes in a 10.5" dual-slot card with a fanless cooling solution designed around a 600W power envelope (the card is designed to be cooled by chassis fans in a rack-mounted server). However, the card can be configured to run at a lower 450W power target to maintain compatibility with more thermally or power-constrained chassis.Swipe to scroll horizontallyAMD MI350X and MI355X specficaitionsSpecifications (PEAK THEORETICAL)AMD Instinct MI350P GPUAMD Instinct MI325X GPUAMD INSTINCT MI350X GPUAMD INSTINCT MI350X PLATFORMAMD INSTINCT MI355X GPUAMD INSTINCT MI355X PLATFORMGPUsInstinct MI350P PCIeInstinct MI325X OAMInstinct MI350X OAM8 x Instinct MI350X OAMInstinct MI355X OAM8 x Instinct MI355X OAMGPU ArchitectureCDNA 4CDNA 3CDNA 4 CDNA 4 CDNA 4 CDNA 4 Dedicated Memory Size144 GB HBM3E256 GB HBM3E288 GB HBM3E2.3 TB HBM3E288 GB HBM3E2.3 TB HBM3EMemory Bandwidth4 TB/s6 TB/s8 TB/s8 TB/s per OAM8 TB/s8 TB/s per OAMFP64 Performance36 TFLOPsRow 4 - Cell 2 72 TFLOPs577 TFLOPs78.6 TFLOPS628.8 TFLOPsFP16 Performance2.3 PFLOPS2.61 PFLOPS4.6 PFLOPS36.8 PFLOPS5 PFLOPS40.2 PFLOPSFP8 Performance4.6 PFLOPS5.22 PFLOPS9.2 PFLOPs73.82 PFLOPs10.1 PFLOPs80.5 PFLOPsFP6 PerformanceRow 7 - Cell 1 Row 7 - Cell 2 18.45 PFLOPS147.6 PFLOPS20.1 PFLOPS161 PFLOPSFP4 Performance*Row 8 - Cell 1 Row 8 - Cell 2 18.45 PFLOPS147.6 PFLOPS20.1 PFLOPS161 PFLOPSThe card's specs are exactly half of what AMD's high-end MI350X and MI355X AI GPUs offer. The MI350P runs off of AMD's CDNA4 architecture and is built on TSMC's 3nm and 6nm FinFET process. The GPU comes with 8,192 cores, 128 CUs, 512 Matrix Cores, and has a 2.2GHz max clock speed. The GPU is paired to 144GB of HBM3E memory with 4TB/s of bandwidth, and a 128MB last-level cache. You may like ISSCC 2026: AMD discloses how the Instinct MI355X doubled per-CU throughput despite lower compute unit count Huawei unveils new "Atlas 350" AI accelerator with 1.56 PFLOPS of FP4 compute & up to 112 GB of HBM AMD's Enterprise CPU and GPU roadmap: Venice, Verano, Zen 6, Helios, and CDNA Latest Videos FromJust like the MI350X and MI355X, the MI350P offers native support for lower-precision MXFP6 and MXFP4 to accelerate LLMs. Up to eight MI350P cards can be paired together in a single system, allowing data centers to scale performance based on how many cards are used. The MI350P is geared towards small, medium, and large AI workloads surrounding inference and RAG pipelines. AMD claims the GPU is the fastest enterprise PCIe card with an estimated 2,299 TFLOPs and 4,600 peak TFLOPs of performance using MXFP4.The introduction of the MI350P finally gives AMD a proper competitor to Nvidia's fastest PCIe AI accelerator, currently the H200 NVL. The MI350P is based on a newer architecture and edges out the H200 NVL in performance, featuring 20% better FP64, 43% better FP16, and 39% better FP8 theoretical compute performance.Image 1 of 17(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)Nvidia has not announced a PCIe version of its latest B200 Blackwell GPUs running HBM memory, so for now, AMD will have the most bleeding-edge AI accelerator that fits in a PCIe form factor. It remains to be seen how widely adopted AMD's new card will be, given Nvidia's hold on the market with CUDA. But AMD is working to improve its competing ROCm software stack, as the GPU maker explained to us at CES 2026.Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
items.author: Aaron Klotz
items.titleZh: AMD发布MI350P PCIe AI加速卡，配备144GB HBM3E内存——FP16和FP8理论算力比竞品英伟达H200 NVL快约40%
items.descriptionZh:
AMD推出了Instinct MI350系列的新成员——MI350P AI加速器，这是该系列首款采用PCIe规格的GPU，专为现有风冷服务器设计，旨在提供“即插即用”的升级方案。MI350P采用10.5英寸双槽无风扇显卡设计，依靠机架式服务器的机箱风扇散热，功耗墙为600W，但也可配置为450W以兼容散热或供电受限的机箱。该卡基于AMD最新的CDNA 4架构，采用台积电3nm和6nm FinFET工艺。具体规格上，MI350P拥有8192个核心、128个计算单元（CUs）、512个矩阵核心，最大时钟频率2.2GHz，配备144GB HBM3E内存，带宽达4TB/s，以及128MB的末级缓存。其性能参数恰好是高端MI350X和MI355X AI GPU的一半。

在核心技术方面，MI350P继承了MI350X和MI355X的特点，原生支持低精度MXFP6和MXFP4格式，专门用于加速大语言模型（LLM）的推理。这种对低精度计算的原生支持是提升AI推理吞吐量和能效的关键创新。用户可以将最多8块MI350P卡组合在一个系统中，通过多卡并联实现性能的线性扩展。AMD宣称，在MXFP4精度下，MI350P的峰值性能估计可达2,299 TFLOPs，理论峰值更是高达4,600 TFLOPs，使其成为“最快的企业级PCIe卡”。

与竞品对比，MI350P的推出直接对标英伟达目前最快的PCIe AI加速器——H200 NVL。尽管H200 NVL基于HBM3e内存，但MI350P基于更新的CDNA 4架构，在理论计算性能上全面胜出：FP64性能高出20%，FP16性能高出43%，FP8性能高出39%。更重要的是，英伟达尚未宣布其最新基于Blackwell架构的B200 GPU的PCIe版本（B200采用的是HBM内存），因此在PCIe规格的AI加速器市场中，MI350P是目前技术最前沿的产品。

技术影响与应用场景上，MI350P主要面向中小型到大型AI工作负载，特别是推理（Inference）和检索增强生成（RAG）流水线。其PCIe外形使得现有数据中心无需更换整机架构即可升级AI算力，大幅降低了部署门槛。然而，其市场成败仍取决于AMD能否继续优化其ROCm软件栈，以打破Nvidia CUDA生态的垄断。正如AMD在2025年CES上所强调的，ROCm的持续改进将是吸引数据中心客户、将MI350P的硬件优势转化为实际应用竞争力的关键。总体而言，MI350P为AI推理市场提供了一个更灵活、兼容性更强且性能强劲的PCIe加速选项。

items.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
items.description:
Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. 
Elon Musk, his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman have already testified before the jury. Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, took the stand on Wednesday, and the courtroom also watched former OpenAI CTO Mura Murati’s videotaped deposition.
Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is scheduled to appear on Monday, May 11th, with OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever lined up to testify after that.

Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.

Plaintiff
Elon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAI
Steven Molo — lead counsel for plaintiff
Jared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family office
Shivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with Musk
Defendant

Greg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounder 
Ilya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounder
Judge
Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judge

items.titleZh: 马斯克与奥尔特曼就OpenAI未来法庭之争实时更新
items.descriptionZh:
一场可能重塑人工智能行业格局的高风险诉讼正在美国加州联邦法院进行：OpenAI联合创始人、现任CEO萨姆·奥特曼与另一位联合创始人、特斯拉及xAI创始人埃隆·马斯克正式对簿公堂。这场审判的核心争议在于，马斯克指控奥特曼和OpenAI背离了公司创立时的非营利使命——开发“造福人类”的人工智能，转而以营利为目的，尤其是通过其明星产品ChatGPT追逐商业利益。马斯克本人、他的家族办公室负责人贾里德·伯查尔以及OpenAI另一位联合创始人格雷格·布罗克曼已经出庭作证。前OpenAI董事会成员、与马斯克共同育有四个孩子的希冯·齐里斯于周三（5月7日）出庭，法庭还播放了前OpenAI首席技术官米拉·穆拉蒂的视频证词。微软CEO萨提亚·纳德拉将于5月11日下周一出庭，其后OpenAI联合创始人、前首席科学家伊利亚·苏茨克维也将作证。

马斯克是OpenAI的原始联合创始人之一，他在2015年共同出资创立了这家公司。他认为，奥特曼和布罗克曼通过承诺坚持非营利、开源、造福人类的愿景，诱使他投入资金和资源；但后来OpenAI转向了商业化和闭源模式，特别是与微软建立了深度合作关系，这违背了创始协议。马斯克声称自己被“欺骗”了。奥特曼和OpenAI一方则坚决否认这一指控，称该诉讼“毫无根据，是出于嫉妒而试图阻挠竞争对手的恶意之举”，其真实目的是帮助马斯克自己的公司（SpaceX、xAI以及X平台）——xAI已经推出了ChatGPT的竞品Grok。OpenAI在庭审文件中强调，公司始终在调整使命的具体实现方式，但核心目标未变，而且马斯克本人曾支持过营利化转型。

在诉讼请求中，马斯克要求法院移除奥特曼和布罗克曼在OpenAI的领导职位，并禁止OpenAI继续以“公益公司”（public benefit corporation）的架构运营——他认为这种架构掩盖了其营利本质，违反了非营利宗旨。此外，如果马斯克胜诉，他要求OpenAI的非营利实体获得高达1500亿美元的损害赔偿（这是他索赔金额的上限）。

从各方角色来看，本案的关键人物包括：原告方——马斯克、其首席律师史蒂文·莫洛、家族办公室负责人伯查尔、前董事会成员齐里斯；被告方——奥特曼、其首席律师威廉·萨维特、OpenAI总裁布罗克曼、前首席科学家苏茨克维；主审法官是伊冯·冈萨雷斯·罗杰斯（YGR）。微软CEO纳德拉的证词将至关重要，因为微软是OpenAI的最大投资者和商业合作伙伴，其表态将直接影响法庭对OpenAI“是否背离非营利初心”的判断。

这场审判的最终结果将直接决定OpenAI的公司治理结构和未来发展路径。如果马斯克胜诉，OpenAI可能被迫剥离营利部门、重建非营利董事会，甚至终止与微软的部分合作；如果奥特曼胜诉，OpenAI将继续以现有模式运营，但公众对AI安全与商业利益之间平衡的质疑会更加强烈。无论结果如何，这一案件都会成为人工智能行业监管与伦理讨论的标志性事件。

items.title: Lian Li’s new DK07 Wood motorized standing desk doubles as a dual-PC chassis with support for E-ATX systems and massive cooling setups — five new wood-finished models are available now
items.description: The updated version adds a dark walnut finish while retaining support for dual systems, large GPUs, and extensive cooling setups.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter PC case and accessories manufacturer Lian Li has announced a new variant for its DK07 motorized standing desk. The company has retained the original launch price of $1,399.99, and the desk is available starting today through Amazon, Newegg, Caseking, Overclockers UK, and SCAN.Originally launched back in 2024, the updated version now comes in a dark walnut wood finish with a tempered glass panel and enough internal space to accommodate single or dual E-ATX systems.Check out the Lian Li DK07 Wood on AmazonCheck out the Lian Li DK07 Wood on NeweggCheck out the Lian Li DK07 Wood on CasekingCheck out the Lian Li DK07 Wood on Overclockers UKCheck out the Lian Li DK07 Wood on SCANApart from the new tabletop finish, the new DK07 Wood offers features similar to the original, including an adjustable height ranging from 676 millimeters (26.61 inches) to 1,162 millimeters (45.7 inches), along with three customizable preset positions. For accessibility, the desk comes with a sliding front panel to lift the tempered glass top when accessing internal components. Additionally, the removable modular motherboard trays make the building process more convenient, allowing users to install and prepare components outside the desk before placing the system into the chassis.Image 1 of 12(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)The desk supports both horizontal and vertical GPU orientations, with clearance of up to 383mm in length. In terms of power supply, one can install up to two full-size 220mm PSUs with up to 10x 3.5” HDD and six 2.5” SSDs. There’s plenty of cooling options as well, including support for a 480mm radiator or up to four 360mm radiators and 17 x 120mm fans in a single system setup, or up to two 360mm radiators and 12 x 120mm fans in a dual system setup. You also get dedicated front panel I/O for each system, which includes power and reset buttons, two USB 3.1 Type-C ports, two USB 3.0 Type-A, and a mic/audio jack. Other notable features include a built-in wireless charging pad, two drawers, internal cable organizers, dual cable pass-through holes for peripherals, and a rear cable tray.Latest Videos FromLian Li’s DK07 standing desk seems quite the practical solution as it not only saves on space but also doubles as a chassis for two PCs with all the proper organization one could ask for, and even a tempered glass panel to show off your premium hardware. In more than one way, it makes more sense than it initially sounds, especially at $1,399.99. Considering the amount of functionality packed into the desk, the pricing feels somewhat reasonable when compared to buying a premium standing desk and a pair of enthusiast-grade PC cases separately. That said, it remains a niche product aimed at enthusiasts.Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds. items.titleZh: 联力新款DK07木纹电动升降桌，可兼作双PC机箱，支持E-ATX系统及大型散热配置——现有五款全新木纹版本可供选择
items.descriptionZh:
PC机箱和配件制造商联力（Lian Li）为其DK07电动升降桌推出了全新变体版本——DK07 Wood。该产品保留了原始1399.99美元（约合人民币1万元）的定价，即日起通过亚马逊、新蛋、Caseking、Overclockers UK和SCAN等渠道开售。DK07最初于2024年发布，此次更新的核心变化在于桌面采用深色胡桃木饰面，并配备钢化玻璃顶板，内部空间足以容纳单路或双路E-ATX系统。

技术层面，DK07 Wood延续了原版的核心设计理念：将电动升降桌与高端PC机箱合二为一。其高度调节范围为676毫米至1162毫米（约26.6至45.7英寸），支持三个自定义预设位置的记忆存储。为方便硬件安装与维护，桌子配备可滑动的前面板，用于升起钢化玻璃顶盖；可拆卸的模块化主板托盘让用户能在桌外完成组件预装，随后整体放入桌内，显著简化了装机流程。

性能参数方面，DK07 Wood支持水平和垂直两种GPU安装方向，显卡限长383毫米。电源部分可容纳最多两个全尺寸220毫米电源。存储方面，最多可安装10个3.5英寸机械硬盘和6个2.5英寸固态硬盘。散热扩展能力是最大亮点：单系统配置下，可支持一块480毫米水冷排或最多四块360毫米水冷排，并容纳17个120毫米风扇；双系统配置下，则可支持最多两块360毫米水冷排和12个120毫米风扇。每个系统均配有独立的前面板I/O接口，包括电源和重启按钮、两个USB 3.1 Type-C端口、两个USB 3.0 Type-A端口以及一组麦克风/音频插孔。此外，桌子还内置了无线充电板、两个抽屉、内部线缆管理器、用于外设的双穿线孔以及后部线缆托盘，细节设计相当周全。

从应用场景来看，联力DK07 Wood主打节省空间与高度整合，尤其适合需要同时运行两台高性能PC的发烧友或内容创作者。它既是一张兼顾健康办公的升降桌，又是一个带有钢化玻璃侧透、可展示硬件的双系统机箱。相较于单独购买一张优质电动升降桌再加两个发烧级机箱的总成本，1399.99美元的定价显得较为合理。不过，这种将电脑桌与机箱深度绑定的设计仍属小众产品，主要面向对桌面整洁度和双系统布线有极致追求的硬件爱好者。

items.title: Motherboard sales 'collapse' by more than 25% as chipmakers strangle enthusiast PC market to build more AI chips — Asus projected to sell 5 million fewer boards in 2025, Gigabyte, MSI, and ASRock also expected to see reduced sales numbers
items.description: The combined sales of the four biggest motherboard manufacturers are expected to fall by at least 28% in 2026, with the entire industry selling 11.7 million less units due to increasing PC component costs.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 14 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Motherboard sales are now collapsing amid unprecedented shortages fueled by AI, causing prices for many major PC components to rise across the board during the past six months, with memory modules and storage drives leading the way. Now those shortages are being exacerbated by chipmakers, like Nvidia, Intel, and AMD, reducing production of consumer chips so they can manufacture more AI processors. The AI infrastructure buildout is also causing shortages for Intel and AMD CPUs (and even high-end Macs), especially as interest in agentic AI is going through the roof.Because of this, users who don’t have deep pockets are putting off upgrading their PCs and holding on to their current devices longer. Motherboard manufacturers have begun to feel the effects of these delayed purchases, with Digitimes [machine translated] reporting that the four major firms are revising their target sales downward.Asus, which sold 15 million motherboards in 2025, has only shipped a little more than 5 million in the first half of 2026. It’s expected that the company will have to push hard for it to even move 10 million units by the end of the year, marking a 33% decrease in sales year-on-year. Gigabyte and MSI sold 11.5 million and 11 million motherboards last year, respectively. However, both companies have revised their internal forecasts for 2026 to 9 million (Gigabyte) and 8.4 million (MSI), a 22% drop for the former and a 24% contraction for the latter. You may like PC makers face shortages of Intel and AMD CPUs that stretch up to six months Brace for a barren landscape of new hardware launches, as AI demand reshapes the world of consumer electronics Entry-level PC market to ‘disappear’ by 2028 Latest Videos FromASRock will be hardest hit by the situation, with the company’s shipments projected to fall by 37%, from 4.3 million in 2025 to just 2.7 million by the end of the year. This marks a contraction of 28% for the overall motherboard market, at least for the big four manufacturers.AI’s demand for memory, storage, and processors is the primary driver for this drop in sales. Shortages that have been caused by their massive purchases have forced PC builders and enthusiasts to fight over a smaller pie of PC components, resulting in higher overall prices for these components.Aside from this, AMD continues to use the AM5 socket for its latest processors, while Intel's Nova Lake, which will reportedly use LGA 1954, isn’t available until later this year. The situation is further compounded by Nvidia not releasing a refreshed RTX 50 Super series this year, while rumors claim that the RTX 60 series will not debut until 2028. This confluence of factors is discouraging PC builders from upgrading their current systems.Despite this drop in sales, these companies aren’t exactly struggling. Asus, Gigabyte, and ASRock have pivoted some of their production towards AI servers, allowing them to capture some of the investments that hyperscalers are generously pouring into their data centers. But if you’re planning to build a completely new PC from scratch, you might be able to find good deals on motherboard combos, especially as retailers are keen on getting their inventories moving. Although these discounts might not be enough to offset the increased costs of memory, storage, and, to some extent, processors, it will at least save you a few dollars as you navigate the current chip crisis.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 主板销量“暴跌”超过25%，芯片制造商为生产更多AI芯片而牺牲发烧友PC市场——华硕预计2025年减少500万块主板出货量，技嘉、微星及华擎预计也将出现销量下滑
items.descriptionZh:
人工智能基础设施的疯狂扩张正在深刻重塑消费电子市场，一场由AI芯片需求引发的连锁短缺危机已波及PC主板行业。过去六个月里，内存模块和存储驱动器价格全面上涨，而NVIDIA、英特尔和AMD等芯片制造商为了生产更多AI处理器，大幅削减了消费级芯片的产量，进一步加剧了短缺。与此同时，对Agentic AI（自主智能体）的兴趣激增，导致英特尔和AMD的CPU（甚至高端Mac）也面临供应紧张，普通用户被迫推迟更换PC，长期持有现有设备。

主板制造商首当其冲。据Digitimes报道，四大主板厂商纷纷下调销售目标：华硕在2025年售出1500万块主板，但2026年上半年仅出货500多万块，预计全年勉强达到1000万块，同比下滑33%；技嘉去年售出1150万块，今年内部目标下调至900万块（跌22%）；微星从1100万块降至840万块（跌24%）；而华擎受影响最严重，预计从430万块骤降至270万块，降幅达37%。这意味着四大厂商合计主板市场将收缩约28%。

销量暴跌的核心原因是AI对内存、存储和处理器的巨大需求。大规模采购造成的短缺迫使PC组装者和发烧友在更小的组件蛋糕中争夺，推高了整体价格。此外，技术层面也缺乏升级动力：AMD继续使用AM5插槽，英特尔即将发布的Nova Lake（采用LGA 1954）要等到今年晚些时候，而NVIDIA今年不推出RTX 50 Super系列，甚至RTX 60系列据传要到2028年才会登场——多重因素叠加让用户升级意愿降至冰点。

不过，这些厂商并未陷入困境。华硕、技嘉和华擎已将部分生产线转向AI服务器，成功捕获了超大规模云厂商为数据中心投入的巨额投资。对于想从零组装新PC的用户，目前主板套装常能遇到折扣——零售商急于清库存——虽然这些优惠可能不足以抵消内存、存储和处理器的涨价，但至少能在芯片危机中节省一些成本。消费者需要更理性地权衡升级时机，等待更好的购买窗口。

items.title: Musk's SpaceX has rented out access to its supercomputer's 220,000 Nvidia GPUs and 300 megawatts of AI compute power to rival Anthropic — Musk says “No one set off my evil detector,” Antrhropic also interested in orbital data centers
items.description: Anthropic has signed a deal with SpaceX to access the latter's massive data center, which has over 220,000 Nvidia GPUs and 300 megawatts of computing power.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Anthropic announced in a press release on Wednesday that it has signed a deal with Elon Musk’s SpaceX to use the company's massive Colossus 1 data center. According to a corresponding announcement from SpaceXAI, the deal will give Anthropic access to all of the massive supercomputer. That's over 222,000 Nvidia GPUs — including powerful H100 and H200 chips alongside next-generation GB200 accelerator systems — and 300 megawatts plus of compute power.Anthropic says this additional capacity will go toward improving the experience for paid Claude users — Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers — via three key changes. Effective yesterday, Claude Code’s five-hour rate limits have doubled for all paid tiers. The company has also removed the peak hours limit reduction for Pro and Max. Lastly, it has “considerably” raised the API rate limits, the volume of requests developers can make, for Claude Opus models.The announcement highlighted other similar agreements with Amazon, Google, and Microsoft, all aimed at building gigawatts of additional capacity. Anthropic also expressed interest in partnering with SpaceX to “multiple gigawatts of orbital AI compute capacity, basically data centers in space, as, according to SpaceX, “The compute required to train and operate the next generation of these systems is outpacing what terrestrial power, land, and cooling can deliver on the timelines that matter.” You may like Sam Altman fires back at Elon Musk's proposal for space-based data centers, says orbiting data centers 'ridiculous' for now Anthropic promises to pay for electricity price increases due to it's AI data centers OpenAI has effectively abandoned first-party Stargate data centers in favor of more flexible deals Latest Videos FromThe Colossus 1 deal means that the whole first-generation cluster, originally built to power xAI’s own Grok models, is now powering one of its direct AI rivals, as the company focuses on building Colossus 2. In an X post, Musk said he gave the green light to lease Colossus 1 to Anthropic after spending time with senior members of the company to “understand what they do to ensure Claude is good for humanity.” He claimed he “was impressed,” saying, “No one set off my evil detector.” The statement is a stark reversal of comments Musk made earlier this year about Anthropic, when he called Claude “misanthropic and evil."In the press release, Anthropic also signaled that its compute expansion strategy extends far beyond raw processing power. The company said future infrastructure deployments will increasingly target international regions, such as Europe and Asia, to meet growing enterprise demand for local data residency and regulatory compliance, particularly in sectors like healthcare, finance, and government. It also stressed that future capacity partnerships will prioritize politically stable, democratic countries with secure AI supply chains, while exploring ways to offset increases in electricity costs and reinvest in communities hosting the data centers.In addition to the compute deal announcement, Anthropic also unveiled on Wednesday a new Claude feature called 'dreaming,' designed to help its AI agents improve themselves between sessions. The feature allows Claude-powered agents to review previous work, identify recurring patterns and mistakes, and reorganize memory files that contain user preferences and contextual information, enabling the system to refine its behavior over time rather than starting each session from scratch. items.titleZh: 马斯克的SpaceX将其超级计算机的22万块英伟达GPU和300兆瓦AI算力租给了竞争对手Anthropic——马斯克表示“没有人触发我的邪恶探测器”，Anthropic也对轨道数据中心感兴趣
items.descriptionZh:
Anthropic 在周三发布的一则公告中宣布，已与埃隆·马斯克的 SpaceX 签署协议，将使用其位于田纳西州的 Colossus 1 数据中心。根据 SpaceXAI 的相应公告，这笔交易让 Anthropic 获得了整个巨型超算的全部使用权。该超算拥有超过 22.2 万块 Nvidia GPU，其中包括性能强劲的 H100 和 H200 芯片，以及下一代 GB200 加速器系统，整体算力达到 300 兆瓦以上。

Anthropic 表示，这笔新增的算力将用于通过三项关键改动来改善付费 Claude 用户（包括 Pro、Max、Team 和 Enterprise 订阅者）的体验。自昨日（周三）起，所有付费层级用户使用的 Claude Code 工具的五小时速率限制已翻倍。公司还取消了 Pro 和 Max 用户原有的高峰时段限制减免。最后，针对 Claude Opus 模型，Anthropic 已“大幅”提高了 API 速率限制，即开发者可以发起的请求数量。

公告还提及了 Anthropic 与亚马逊、谷歌和微软达成的类似协议，这些合作都旨在建设千兆瓦量级的额外算力。此外，Anthropic 表示有兴趣与 SpaceX 合作建设“多千兆瓦的轨道 AI 计算能力，即太空数据中心”。SpaceX 认为，“训练和运行下一代 AI 系统所需算力的增长速度，已经超出了地面供电、土地和冷却能在相关时间线上所能提供的支持。”

值得注意的是，Colossus 1 数据中心最初是为 xAI 训练自己的 Grok 模型而建造的，如今随着 xAI 正在全力建设 Colossus 2，这座第一代集群已全部转用于支撑其直接竞争对手 Anthropic 的运算。马斯克在 X 平台上发帖称，他在与 Anthropic 的高管们共度时光，了解他们“如何确保 Claude 对人类有益”之后，才批准将 Colossus 1 租赁给 Anthropic。他表示自己“印象深刻”，并称“没有人的行为触发我的邪恶探测器”。这一表态与马斯克今年早些时候对 Anthropic 的评论形成鲜明对比——当时他曾称 Claude“反人类且邪恶”。

在新闻稿中，Anthropic 还暗示其算力扩张战略远不止于增加原始处理能力。公司表示，未来的基础设施部署将越来越多地瞄准欧洲和亚洲等国际区域，以满足企业客户对本地数据驻留和监管合规日益增长的需求，尤其是在医疗、金融和政府等领域。Anthropic 还强调，未来的算力合作伙伴关系将优先考虑政治稳定、拥有安全 AI 供应链的民主国家，同时探索抵消数据中心电力成本上涨的途径，并对托管数据中心社区进行再投资。

除了算力合作的宣布，Anthropic 同日还推出了 Claude 的一项名为“dreaming”（做梦）的新功能，旨在帮助其 AI 智能体在会话之间实现自我改进。该功能允许基于 Claude 的智能体回顾之前的工作，识别重复出现的模式和错误，并重组包含用户偏好和上下文信息的记忆文件，从而使系统能够随着时间推移不断优化自身行为，而无需每次会话都从零开始。

items.title: Nvidia’s ISP piracy defense backfires as judge refuses to dismiss copyright lawsuit over more than 197,000 pirated books — scripts in NeMo Framework allegedly ‘have no other purpose’ than to speed up infringement
items.description: Nvidia said that its NeMo Megatron Framework has non-infringing uses and that it's not liable for any piracy that its users might do.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 7 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter U.S. District Judge Jon Tigar has denied Nvidia's request to dismiss a copyright infringement case filed against it, arguing that it’s not liable for how clients use its AI-powered NeMo Megatron Framework. According to TorrentFreak, Nvidia is asking the court to dismiss the direct copyright infringement claims that are connected to its use of the Bibliotik eBook torrent tracker, the Books3 dataset, and 'The Pile' dataset for language modeling. Nvidia then cited the Cox vs. Sony ruling, where the U.S. Supreme Court ruled that a service provider is not liable for any piracy that its users might carry out.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowNvidia said that its NeMo Megatron Framework has significant “non-infringing uses” and that it did not promote it as a piracy tool. This should fall under Justice Clarence Thomas’ decision saying, “Under our precedents, a company is not liable as a copyright infringer for merely providing a service to the general public with knowledge that it will used by some to infringe copyrights.” Unfortunately for the company, Judge Tigar disagreed with its argument, saying that it’s not the framework, but specific scripts within it that violated copyright rules.He said that these were intended to make it easier for users to automatically download and preprocess The Pile dataset, which the complainants said allegedly contained copyrighted work. “The scripts are alleged to have no other purpose than to speed up the process of infringement, unlike the digital video recorder systems at issue in Sony Corp. or the internet service provided in Cox,” Judge Tigar wrote. Bibliotik is a private eBook torrent tracker, which allegedly contains over 197,000 books. It was then included in the Books3 dataset, which itself was included in the 800+ gigabyte The Pile dataset. The Pile was then used for training Nvidia’s AI LLMs, resulting in several authors filing a class action lawsuit against the company for copyright infringement. You may like Nvidia says it didn't use pirated books to train its AI models Class action alleges Nvidia hid more than $1B crypto-GPU income within its gaming revenues Nvidia's own DLSS 5 announcement video gets taken down by YouTube in Italy due to a copyright strike Latest Videos FromThere have been previous cases of copyright infringement related to AI companies scraping data for training their models. Aside from this case against Nvidia, Meta has also been facing a similar lawsuit since last year. It even defended itself by saying that using pirated material is legal if you don’t seed content. Google has even been pushing to have AI scraping tagged as fair use, saying that it wants “copyright systems that enable appropriate and fair use of copyrighted content to enable the training of AI models in Australia on a broad and diverse range of data while supporting workable opt-outs for entities that prefer their data not to be trained in using AI systems.”With this decision, the authors’ class action against Nvidia is set to move forward, and we will likely hear more details as the case progresses. We don’t have a date yet for when the next hearing will be, though. Still, we expect this to be a multi-year battle as the AI giant battles it out with allegedly infringed writers. items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 英伟达的互联网服务提供商盗版辩护适得其反，法官拒绝驳回涉及超19.7万本盗版书籍的版权诉讼——NeMo框架中的脚本被
items.descriptionZh:
美国联邦地区法官Jon Tigar驳回了英伟达（Nvidia）试图撤销一起版权侵权诉讼的请求。这起诉讼指控英伟达的AI框架NeMo Megatron Framework被用于训练其大语言模型（LLMs），过程中使用了包含受版权保护作品的盗版数据集。法官裁定，英伟达不能以“仅提供服务”为由免除责任，因为其框架内包含的特定脚本旨在帮助用户自动下载和预处理被指控侵权的数据集，这超出了普通“服务提供者”的范畴。

**背景与上下文**  
这起集体诉讼由多位作者提起，指控英伟达未经授权使用了其作品来训练AI模型。核心争议涉及三个数据集：Bibliotik、Books3和The Pile。Bibliotik是一个私有的电子书种子追踪网站，据称包含超过19.7万本书；这些书被整合进Books3数据集，而Books3又被纳入规模超过800GB的The Pile数据集。英伟达的NeMo Megatron框架正是通过处理The Pile等数据集来训练其AI大语言模型。原告认为，这构成了直接版权侵权。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
NeMo Megatron是英伟达推出的一个用于训练和部署大型语言模型的框架，它本身是一个通用的AI开发平台，理论上可以支持多种非侵权用途。然而，诉讼指出，该框架内包含一些特定脚本（scripts），这些脚本的功能是“自动化下载和预处理The Pile数据集”。法官认为，这些脚本的唯一目的就是加速盗版过程，没有其他合法用途。这与英伟达所引用的“索尼诉环球影业案”中录像机（VCR）或“Cox诉索尼案”中的互联网服务不同——这些技术工具本身具有广泛的合法用途，而涉案脚本本质上是为侵权量身定做的。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
文章没有提供具体的模型性能参数，但提及了数据集的规模：Bibliotik包含约19.7万本书，Books3是其中一部分，而The Pile整体超过800GB。这种规模的数据集通常用于训练拥有数十亿甚至数百亿参数的LLM。相比之下，英伟达在诉讼中试图援引美国最高法院的判例：在Cox案中，法院裁定服务提供商不对用户实施的盗版行为承担责任，前提是服务本身具有“实质性的非侵权用途”。但法官Tigar指出，本案中的脚本并不具备这样的实质性合法用途，因此不适用该判例。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
此案的裁决具有深远影响。它明确了AI公司在提供训练框架或工具时，不能仅仅通过声称“有非侵权用途”来规避责任，特别是当工具中包含了专门用于获取侵权数据集的脚本时。这为AI领域的版权纠纷设立了新的法律边界。从应用场景看，NeMo Megatron本意是帮助企业高效训练自己的大语言模型，用于自然语言处理、对话系统、内容生成等合法领域。但一旦框架被设计为便于加载盗版数据集，其开发者的责任就可能被追究。

**当前进展与未来展望**  
目前，这起集体诉讼已被允许继续推进。英伟达此前还面临另一项相关指控：去年有作者起诉Meta（Facebook母公司）同样使用了Books3等数据集训练其LLaMA模型，而Meta当时辩护称，如果只是下载而不“做种”（seeding）就不算侵权。谷歌也在游说将AI抓取训练数据认定为“合理使用”，并希望在澳大利亚等地区推动相应的版权系统改革。法官Tigar的裁决意味着作者一方的指控将进入实质审理阶段，下一次听证日期尚未确定。预计这将是一场持续多年的法律拉锯战，其结果可能重塑AI公司使用版权数据训练模型的法律边界。

items.title: After a $16 billion Stargate AI data center was built despite being voted down, Michigan towns rush to block new buildouts — massive facility will suck 1.4 Gigawatts of energy to power ChatGPT
items.description: Backlash now covers county resolutions, bipartisan state legislation, and a regional water authority refusing to serve proposed facilities.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 4 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter After a developer sued a township, OpenAI and Oracle's massive new Stargate data center, which will consume 1.4 Gigawatts of electricity, is now underway despite a township's vote to reject the new facility, sparking backlash. As a result, at least 19 Michigan municipalities have enacted moratoriums on new data center development since a $16 billion Stargate facility for Oracle and OpenAI was pushed through in Saline Township over near-unanimous local opposition last year, according to Bridge Michigan. The backlash now covers county resolutions, bipartisan state legislation, and a regional water authority refusing to serve proposed facilities.The Saline project advanced despite a 4-1 vote by the township board to reject Related Digital's rezoning request in September. The developer sued within two days, alleging exclusionary zoning, and the township settled within weeks. Residents secured roughly $14 million in community benefits, including funding for the local fire department, farmland preservation, and environmental restrictions. “I think the plan was to move as fast as possible—so by the time anyone challenged it, they could say it was too far along to stop,” said one resident speaking to Forbes recently.Michigan has since become a focal point for data center opposition. At least 19 municipalities across the state had enacted moratoriums on new data center development as of early February. Washtenaw County commissioners passed a resolution in March supporting local moratoriums, and bipartisan state legislation proposed a one-year statewide pause, though both Governor Whitmer and House Speaker Matt Hall have opposed that measure. You may like OpenAI's massive Stargate data center canceled as firm can't reach terms with Oracle, operator struggles with reliability issues Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center Stargate AI data centers for OpenAI reportedly delayed by squabbles between partners Latest Videos FromRelated Digital and Blackstone announced last month that they secured financing for the Saline campus, purpose-built for Oracle as part of the Stargate AI infrastructure project with OpenAI. Its price tag has more than doubled from the $7 billion figure cited when it was first announced late last year, though there has been no explanation for the jump. Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer has called the project the largest single investment in state history.The campus consists of three single-story data center buildings delivering more than a gigawatt of compute, with DTE Energy supplying roughly 1.4 GW of electricity using existing grid resources supplemented by battery storage funded entirely by Oracle. Related Digital says the arrangement will generate $300 million in savings for existing DTE ratepayers by spreading fixed grid maintenance costs across a larger customer base.The facility will use a closed-loop cooling system rather than evaporative cooling, and Related Digital says ongoing water consumption will be comparable to a standard office building. The developer also claims that 750 of the roughly 1,000-acre site will be preserved as open space, farmland, and wetlands.The Michigan Public Service Commission conditionally approved DTE's special contracts to service the facility in December, but the Michigan attorney general's office has appealed that decision. Construction began in November, and Related Digital says the project is on schedule for delivery to Oracle.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIn April, the Ypsilanti Community Utilities Authority approved a 12-month moratorium on supplying water to data centers, blocking service to facilities including a proposed University of Michigan and Los Alamos National Laboratory supercomputing center. The City of Saline, which was sued by Related Digital in October, enacted its own 12-month moratorium in January.Several additional AI data center projects are in the pipeline across southeast Michigan. Anthropic is the intended end user of a proposed hyperscale facility in Lyon Township, while Google is evaluating a one-gigawatt campus in Van Buren Township near Detroit Metropolitan Airport. Developers have identified at least 16 potential data center sites across 10 Michigan counties, Fortune reported. items.titleZh: 在耗资160亿美元的“星门”AI数据中心尽管被投票否决仍建成后，密歇根州城镇急于阻止新的扩建——这座巨型设施将消耗1.4吉瓦电力来驱动ChatGPT
items.descriptionZh:
OpenAI与Oracle共同参与的“星门”（Stargate）超级数据中心项目，尽管在密歇根州萨林镇遭遇了几乎一致的地方反对，却通过开发商的法律诉讼和快速和解强行推进，如今已正式动工。这一耗资从最初70亿美元飙升至160亿美元的项目，预计将消耗1.4吉瓦（GW）电力，成为该州历史上最大的单笔投资。然而，由此引发的民间反弹迅速蔓延，至少19个密歇根城镇已暂停新数据中心开发，部分县议会通过支持暂停的决议，州议会也提出为期一年的全州暂停法案，尽管州长和众议长均表示反对。地方水务机构甚至拒绝向拟建的数据中心供水。

星门数据园区由开发商Related Digital主导，专为Oracle量身打造，作为OpenAI参与的“星门”AI基础设施项目的一部分。园区包含三座单层数据中心建筑，总计算能力超过1吉瓦。电力由DTE能源公司通过现有电网资源提供，并辅以Oracle全额资助的电池储能系统。开发商声称，这一安排将固定电网维护成本分摊到更大的用户群，从而为现有DTE用户节省约3亿美元。在冷却技术上，该项目采用闭环冷却系统，而非传统蒸发冷却，其持续耗水量仅相当于一座普通办公楼，显著降低了对水资源的依赖。园区占地约1000英亩，其中750英亩将被保留为开放空间、农田和湿地。

项目推进过程中充满冲突。去年9月，萨林镇委员会以4比1投票否决了Related Digital的重新分区申请，但开发商两天内即以“排斥性分区”为由起诉镇政府，数周内双方达成和解。居民最终争取到约1400万美元的社区福利，包括当地消防部门资金、农田保护以及环境限制措施。一位居民对《福布斯》表示：“我认为计划就是尽可能快地推进——这样当有人提出挑战时，他们可以说项目已经进行得太深，无法停止了。”2024年12月，密歇根州公共服务委员会有条件批准了DTE为该设施服务的特殊合同，但州总检察长办公室已就此提起上诉。施工于去年11月开始，开发商称项目将按时交付给Oracle。

这场反对浪潮正在重塑密歇根州的数据中心发展格局。截至2025年2月初，全州至少19个市镇已颁布新数据中心开发暂停令。3月，沃什特诺县委员会通过决议，支持地方暂停令；两党州立法机构也提出了为期一年的全州暂停提案，但州长格雷琴·惠特默和众议院议长马特·霍尔均表示反对。此外，伊普西兰蒂社区公用事业局于4月批准了为期12个月的供水暂停令，切断了包括密歇根大学和洛斯阿拉莫斯国家实验室拟建超级计算中心在内的设施的水源。萨林市也在1月实施了为期12个月的暂停令，该市此前曾因类似问题被Related Digital起诉。

密歇根州东南部还有多个AI数据中心项目在筹备中。Anthropic是莱昂镇一座超大规模设施的预期最终用户，而谷歌正在评估底特律大都会机场附近范布伦镇的一个1吉瓦园区。据《财富》杂志报道，开发商已在密歇根州10个县确定了至少16个潜在数据中心站点。这一系列事件凸显了大型AI基础设施建设与地方社区利益、环境资源之间的深刻矛盾，也为美国其他地区的类似项目提供了警示。

items.content: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter exclusively for Verge subscribers about tech, politics, and Washington intrigue. (It’s basically House of Cards, but for nerds.) Not a subscriber yet? You really should become one, and to save you a Google search, here is the direct link to do so! And do you think I should know something? Send it to tina.nguyen+tips@theverge.com.On Monday, The New York Times reported that the White House was considering having the government review AI models before release. To the casual Verge reader, it appeared to be a total reversal in Donald Trump’s policies. For the past year, he had been a vocal champion of pro-industry deregulation, repealing former President Joe Biden’s massive executive order on AI safety, lifting export controls on advanced chips, and signing executive orders that would have legally punished states for passing and enforcing AI laws in the vacuum of federal legislation. Now, the Trump administration has seemingly pulled a 180, demanding federal oversight and vetting of pre-market models.But to Washington, the shift in the White House’s policy was due to three major changes. First, Anthropic’s Mythos has genuinely spooked the national security apparatus, forcing the administration to confront a new threat: the possibility of adversaries using American AI models to attack America’s public and private sectors. Second, other countries are now beginning to lay out their own AI regulations, potentially in a manner that would go against the interests of the United States. (And yes, “destroying a Big Tech data center in a targeted drone strike” is a manner of government AI regulation, but we’ll get to that shortly.)And third, David Sacks was pushed out of his job as the AI and crypto czar, giving Silicon Valley one less mechanism to pitch an industry-friendly, “innovation-at-all-costs” agenda to Trump himself.The definition of political influence can be squishy and amorphous, especially around Donald Trump, who will pick up anyone’s calls and then act on that advice if he feels like it. (Remember when Laura Loomer had control over the National Security Council?) But what’s legally certain is that Sacks, the billionaire venture capitalist and Trump fundraiser in 2024, no longer has the privileges available to him as a special government employee, such as the ability to review sensitive information, to speak on behalf of the White House, or to hold official influence over government employees and agencies.Instead, the “special government employee,” who was supposed to only spend 130 days working in the administration and somehow stuck around for an entire year, actively undermined the administration and torched its relationship with its political allies. During Sacks’ tenure, the White House went beyond simply advocating for less regulation. They tried twice to get Congress to pass a moratorium on state AI laws, and failing that, tried to use an executive order that would grant the Trump admin the powers to sue states passing or enforcing said laws. But his Valley-esque tactics, to say nothing of his attempts to consolidate power over AI policy by boxing out existing agencies, ended up infuriating Republican and MAGA allies, while alienating vast swaths of Trump’s base. (In fact, it was so unsuccessful that when unnamed White House officials recently attempted to pressure certain red states into dropping pending AI legislation, claiming that they were going against Trump’s agenda, four GOP state lawmakers spoke on the record to The Wall Street Journal instead. Then again, if the agenda was just to kill those bills in the cradle, it succeeded.)Even if Sacks hadn’t crashed and burned — to say nothing of publicly criticizing Donald Trump, a man who does not like being criticized, for continuing to wage war against Iran — the job is also getting harder for one part-time employee maintaining ties to the private sector to handle. In recent months, the aperture of America’s AI policy has widened to a scale much, much broader than Sacks’ pro-innovation 2025 remit, into areas where a lack of regulation would be wildly irresponsible: national security and geopolitical stability.A major turning point was the leak of Anthropic’s Mythos, the AI model that was so powerful at finding cybersecurity vulnerabilities that the company, whose reputation hinges on acting more responsibly than its competitors, refused to release it to the public. The possibility of a Mythos-level model becoming commercially available spooked the national security apparatus and the financial industry, and seized the attention of three powerful White House figures: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, and Chief of Staff Susie Wiles.When Bessent and Wiles met with Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei in April, it signaled that not only were they taking the threat seriously, they were now overriding Anthropic’s enemies in the Pentagon, who had, months prior, convinced Trump that Anthropic was “woke” and should be banned for government use.“The national security implications of something like Mythos are hard to deny, and legitimately urgent national security issues are not easy to politicize,” Charlie Bullock, a senior research fellow at the Institute for Law and AI, told The Verge. “Once serious national security people get involved, it’s hard to dismiss or politicize the issue.”In recent weeks, federal agencies that had been boxed out by Sacks are now being given more authority. On Tuesday, the Commerce Department announced that it had designated the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) as the agency that would conduct pre-deployment testing on commercial frontier AI models before release, and had already struck agreements with xAI, Microsoft, and Google DeepMind. CAISI is run by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), which had been gutted by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) last year, but has begun hiring for technical positions.Other countries are flexing their muscles, too, in a way that the United States can’t directly control. The European Union is currently debating a revision to the AI Act, and though the EU countries and the European Parliament were unable to reach an agreement in the most recent negotiations, whatever does end up coming out of that legislation will have a direct impact on how frontier AI models are developed — and possibly in a way that inadvertently acts against America’s business and natsec interests.“[Bessent] really doesn’t like the Europeans,” a tech policy adviser close to the administration told The Verge. In his view, the EU’s proposed privacy regulations would not just hurt American companies, it would also inadvertently allow China to develop faster, and there was historical precedent: “We’ve seen this movie before when it came to broadband, where they tried to do the same thing to American broadband companies. In the end, they were just helping Huawei.”Then there are rogue geopolitical players who simply do not care what Sacks or the American government think. Days after US forces bombed Tehran and killed its religious leader, Iran conducted drone strikes on two AWS data centers in the United Arab Emirates and indirectly damaged a third data center in Bahrain, causing major power outages across the Middle East and damaging critical infrastructure. Weeks later, Iranian state media announced that it would directly target 18 major US tech companies with a presence in the region, including AI heavy hitters like Google, Meta, Microsoft, Palantir, and Nvidia, and has since claimed that it hit an Oracle data center in the UAE. (UAE media later clarified that an Oracle building in Dubai suffered minor damage from falling debris from an aerial drone interception.)“While there’s a lot of politics around it here in the United States, where Maine is [trying] banning them, and [Republican Florida Gov. Ron] DeSantis is talking about banning them, as far as the world is concerned, this is critical infrastructure,” the tech policy adviser close to the administration told me. (DeSantis has proposed empowering towns to reject data center proposals and banning their construction near schools, but has not publicly suggested banning them entirely.) “And that’s why one of the first things the Iranians did was bomb not just one, but two of Amazon’s critical data centers, because they know how important it is.” The damage to AWS’s data centers, which service the entire Middle East, are severe enough that even if the war were to end now, it would take “several months” to resume full operations, according to the company.This is not to say, however, that David Sacks has no influence in the Trump administration: He has Trump’s direct cell, and he’s still a billionaire CEO, which is a better credential in Trump’s eyes than any kind of expertise. The Atlantic’s George Packer recently published a massive feature on Sacks, highlighting his plutocratic tenacity. One can’t really stop a master of the universe from trying to flex every so often. But even when it comes to Trump’s favorite rich guys, Sacks may not even stack up. Last week, Trump hosted a state banquet for King Charles III’s visit to the United States. The guest list included Tim Cook, Jensen Huang, Jeff Bezos, Marc Andreessen, Marc Benioff, corporate leadership from Meta and Alphabet — and no Sacks, who had been present at a previous state banquet held at Windsor Castle in the United Kingdom last year, while he was still at the White House.When I asked a DC insider familiar with state dinner politics if Sacks had been invited, the answer was pretty telling: “Why would he be? He’s not White House inner circle.”And now, Recess.Image via @netcapgirl/Instagram.See you next week.Update, May 7th: Clarified Ron DeSantis’ position on data centers.

items.titleZh: 大卫·萨克斯如何在白宫惨败
items.descriptionZh:
美国白宫对人工智能监管政策出现重大转向：从特朗普此前力推的行业松绑、撤销拜登AI安全行政令、放宽芯片出口管制，转而考虑在AI模型发布前进行政府审查。这一政策逆转背后存在三大关键变化：Anthropic公司的“Mythos”模型令国家安全机构深感不安；其他国家开始制定可能损害美国利益的AI监管规则；以及前AI与加密货币事务官David Sacks被排挤出局，使硅谷失去向特朗普直接兜售“创新至上”议程的渠道。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
Mythos是Anthropic开发的一款前沿AI模型，其核心能力在于自主发现网络安全漏洞。该模型的漏洞挖掘能力极其强大，以至于一贯以“负责任”形象著称的Anthropic拒绝将其公开部署。公司担心，若此类模型落入敌手，可能被用于对美国公共和私营部门发起攻击。这一技术突破彻底改变了华盛顿对AI风险的认知——此前特朗普政府倾向于认为AI监管会扼杀创新，但Mythos的出现证明，缺乏监管的AI可能成为国家安全的直接威胁。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
原文未直接给出Mythos的具体性能参数，但描述其“在寻找网络安全漏洞方面强大到公司拒绝发布”。与之对比，目前商用的前沿模型（如GPT-4、Claude 3等）在漏洞发现能力上尚未达到同等水平。这一能力差异迫使美国财政部、商务部及白宫高层亲自介入，甚至绕过此前敌视Anthropic的五角大楼，直接与公司CEO会晤。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
Mythos级别的模型一旦商用，其影响远超商业领域。在应用场景上，这种模型可被用于：1）国家级网络攻击：识别并利用关键基础设施（电网、金融系统、军事网络）的漏洞；2）企业安全防御：但若被恶意使用，反而会扩大攻击面；3）情报争夺：国家行为体可能利用其窃取对手机密。正是这种双重用途特性，使得特朗普政府不得不从“创新优先”转向“安全审查优先”。

**政策博弈与地缘政治背景**  
- **David Sacks被边缘化**：这位身兼风险投资家与特朗普金主身份的“特殊政府雇员”，原本仅应任职130天，却实际留任一年。他试图推动国会暂停各州AI立法、用行政令威胁起诉通过AI法律的州，甚至排挤其他联邦机构，最终激怒共和党盟友和MAGA基本盘。随着他公开批评特朗普对伊朗政策，其白宫影响力彻底瓦解——特朗普为英国国王查尔斯三世举办国宴时，未邀请Sacks，而库克、黄仁勋、贝佐斯等人悉数在列。

- **新监管架构浮现**：被Sacks排挤的联邦机构重新获得权力。美国商务部已将AI标准与创新中心（CAISI）指定为商业前沿AI模型部署前的测试机构，并已与xAI、微软、谷歌DeepMind达成协议。CAISI由此前被马斯克效率部门（DOGE）重创的美国国家标准与技术研究院（NIST）运营，现已开始招聘技术人员。

- **国际博弈加剧**：欧盟正在修订《AI法案》，谈判虽未达成一致，但最终法案将直接影响前沿模型开发，可能无意中帮助中国（如历史上欧盟对宽带公司的过度监管反而成就了华为）。更激进的案例是伊朗：在美国轰炸德黑兰并杀死宗教领袖后，伊朗用无人机袭击了阿联酋的两个AWS数据中心，并间接破坏巴林的第三个数据中心，导致中东大范围断电和关键基础设施受损。伊朗随后宣布将直接瞄准在美国运营的18家大型科技公司，包括谷歌、Meta、微软、Palantir和英伟达，并声称已击中甲骨文在阿联酋的数据中心。AWS表示，即使战争立即结束，其数据中心也需要“数月”才能恢复全面运营。

**结论**  
尽管Sacks仍拥有特朗普的直接手机号且身为亿万富翁CEO，但他已不再是白宫核心圈子成员。当前美国AI政策已从狭隘的“创新去监管”转向与国家安全、地缘政治、关键基础设施防护结合的复杂格局。Mythos模型泄露事件、伊朗对数据中心的物理攻击、以及欧盟的监管压力，共同迫使特朗普政府正视一个事实：在AI领域，“无监管”本身就是一种危险的政策选择。

history.title: AMD announces MI350P PCIe AI accelerator card with 144GB of HBM3E — roughly 40% faster in FP16 and FP8 theoretical compute compared to Nvidia's H200 NVL competitor
history.description: AMD launches a new MI350P PCIe AI-accelerator card with half the cores and memory of its flagship Instinct MI355X GPU. The new card provides customers with a drop-in upgrade solution for existing air-cooled servers.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD has launched a new member of the MI350-series that comes in a PCIe form factor. The new Instinct MI350P comes with 128 CUs and 144GB of HBM3E memory and is designed to be a drop-in upgrade solution for existing air-cooled servers.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingThe MI350P comes in a 10.5" dual-slot card with a fanless cooling solution designed around a 600W power envelope (the card is designed to be cooled by chassis fans in a rack-mounted server). However, the card can be configured to run at a lower 450W power target to maintain compatibility with more thermally or power-constrained chassis.Swipe to scroll horizontallyAMD MI350X and MI355X specficaitionsSpecifications (PEAK THEORETICAL)AMD Instinct MI350P GPUAMD Instinct MI325X GPUAMD INSTINCT MI350X GPUAMD INSTINCT MI350X PLATFORMAMD INSTINCT MI355X GPUAMD INSTINCT MI355X PLATFORMGPUsInstinct MI350P PCIeInstinct MI325X OAMInstinct MI350X OAM8 x Instinct MI350X OAMInstinct MI355X OAM8 x Instinct MI355X OAMGPU ArchitectureCDNA 4CDNA 3CDNA 4 CDNA 4 CDNA 4 CDNA 4 Dedicated Memory Size144 GB HBM3E256 GB HBM3E288 GB HBM3E2.3 TB HBM3E288 GB HBM3E2.3 TB HBM3EMemory Bandwidth4 TB/s6 TB/s8 TB/s8 TB/s per OAM8 TB/s8 TB/s per OAMFP64 Performance36 TFLOPsRow 4 - Cell 2 72 TFLOPs577 TFLOPs78.6 TFLOPS628.8 TFLOPsFP16 Performance2.3 PFLOPS2.61 PFLOPS4.6 PFLOPS36.8 PFLOPS5 PFLOPS40.2 PFLOPSFP8 Performance4.6 PFLOPS5.22 PFLOPS9.2 PFLOPs73.82 PFLOPs10.1 PFLOPs80.5 PFLOPsFP6 PerformanceRow 7 - Cell 1 Row 7 - Cell 2 18.45 PFLOPS147.6 PFLOPS20.1 PFLOPS161 PFLOPSFP4 Performance*Row 8 - Cell 1 Row 8 - Cell 2 18.45 PFLOPS147.6 PFLOPS20.1 PFLOPS161 PFLOPSThe card's specs are exactly half of what AMD's high-end MI350X and MI355X AI GPUs offer. The MI350P runs off of AMD's CDNA4 architecture and is built on TSMC's 3nm and 6nm FinFET process. The GPU comes with 8,192 cores, 128 CUs, 512 Matrix Cores, and has a 2.2GHz max clock speed. The GPU is paired to 144GB of HBM3E memory with 4TB/s of bandwidth, and a 128MB last-level cache. You may like ISSCC 2026: AMD discloses how the Instinct MI355X doubled per-CU throughput despite lower compute unit count Huawei unveils new "Atlas 350" AI accelerator with 1.56 PFLOPS of FP4 compute & up to 112 GB of HBM AMD's Enterprise CPU and GPU roadmap: Venice, Verano, Zen 6, Helios, and CDNA Latest Videos FromJust like the MI350X and MI355X, the MI350P offers native support for lower-precision MXFP6 and MXFP4 to accelerate LLMs. Up to eight MI350P cards can be paired together in a single system, allowing data centers to scale performance based on how many cards are used. The MI350P is geared towards small, medium, and large AI workloads surrounding inference and RAG pipelines. AMD claims the GPU is the fastest enterprise PCIe card with an estimated 2,299 TFLOPs and 4,600 peak TFLOPs of performance using MXFP4.The introduction of the MI350P finally gives AMD a proper competitor to Nvidia's fastest PCIe AI accelerator, currently the H200 NVL. The MI350P is based on a newer architecture and edges out the H200 NVL in performance, featuring 20% better FP64, 43% better FP16, and 39% better FP8 theoretical compute performance.Image 1 of 17(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)Nvidia has not announced a PCIe version of its latest B200 Blackwell GPUs running HBM memory, so for now, AMD will have the most bleeding-edge AI accelerator that fits in a PCIe form factor. It remains to be seen how widely adopted AMD's new card will be, given Nvidia's hold on the market with CUDA. But AMD is working to improve its competing ROCm software stack, as the GPU maker explained to us at CES 2026.Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors

history.titleZh: AMD发布MI350P PCIe AI加速卡，配备144GB HBM3E内存——FP16和FP8理论算力比竞品英伟达H200 NVL快约40%
history.descriptionZh:
AMD推出了Instinct MI350系列的新成员——MI350P AI加速器，这是该系列首款采用PCIe规格的GPU，专为现有风冷服务器设计，旨在提供“即插即用”的升级方案。MI350P采用10.5英寸双槽无风扇显卡设计，依靠机架式服务器的机箱风扇散热，功耗墙为600W，但也可配置为450W以兼容散热或供电受限的机箱。该卡基于AMD最新的CDNA 4架构，采用台积电3nm和6nm FinFET工艺。具体规格上，MI350P拥有8192个核心、128个计算单元（CUs）、512个矩阵核心，最大时钟频率2.2GHz，配备144GB HBM3E内存，带宽达4TB/s，以及128MB的末级缓存。其性能参数恰好是高端MI350X和MI355X AI GPU的一半。

在核心技术方面，MI350P继承了MI350X和MI355X的特点，原生支持低精度MXFP6和MXFP4格式，专门用于加速大语言模型（LLM）的推理。这种对低精度计算的原生支持是提升AI推理吞吐量和能效的关键创新。用户可以将最多8块MI350P卡组合在一个系统中，通过多卡并联实现性能的线性扩展。AMD宣称，在MXFP4精度下，MI350P的峰值性能估计可达2,299 TFLOPs，理论峰值更是高达4,600 TFLOPs，使其成为“最快的企业级PCIe卡”。

与竞品对比，MI350P的推出直接对标英伟达目前最快的PCIe AI加速器——H200 NVL。尽管H200 NVL基于HBM3e内存，但MI350P基于更新的CDNA 4架构，在理论计算性能上全面胜出：FP64性能高出20%，FP16性能高出43%，FP8性能高出39%。更重要的是，英伟达尚未宣布其最新基于Blackwell架构的B200 GPU的PCIe版本（B200采用的是HBM内存），因此在PCIe规格的AI加速器市场中，MI350P是目前技术最前沿的产品。

技术影响与应用场景上，MI350P主要面向中小型到大型AI工作负载，特别是推理（Inference）和检索增强生成（RAG）流水线。其PCIe外形使得现有数据中心无需更换整机架构即可升级AI算力，大幅降低了部署门槛。然而，其市场成败仍取决于AMD能否继续优化其ROCm软件栈，以打破Nvidia CUDA生态的垄断。正如AMD在2025年CES上所强调的，ROCm的持续改进将是吸引数据中心客户、将MI350P的硬件优势转化为实际应用竞争力的关键。总体而言，MI350P为AI推理市场提供了一个更灵活、兼容性更强且性能强劲的PCIe加速选项。

history.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
history.description:
Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. 
Elon Musk, his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman have already testified before the jury. Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, took the stand on Wednesday, and the courtroom also watched former OpenAI CTO Mura Murati’s videotaped deposition.
Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is scheduled to appear on Monday, May 11th, with OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever lined up to testify after that.

Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.
People to Know
Plaintiff
Elon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAI
Steven Molo — lead counsel for plaintiff
Jared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family office
Shivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with Musk
Defendant
Sam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAI 
William Savitt — lead counsel for defendant
Greg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounder 
Ilya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounder
Judge
Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judge

history.content: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead.Elon Musk, his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman have already testified before the jury. Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, took the stand on Wednesday, and the courtroom also watched former OpenAI CTO Mura Murati’s videotaped deposition.Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is scheduled to appear on Monday, May 11th, with OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever lined up to testify after that.Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.People to KnowPlaintiffElon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAISteven Molo — lead counsel for plaintiffJared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family officeShivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with MuskDefendantSam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAIWilliam Savitt — lead counsel for defendantGreg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounderIlya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounderJudgeYvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judgeHere’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:

history.titleZh: 马斯克与奥尔特曼就OpenAI未来法庭之争实时更新
history.descriptionZh:
一场可能重塑人工智能行业格局的高风险诉讼正在美国加州联邦法院进行：OpenAI联合创始人、现任CEO萨姆·奥特曼与另一位联合创始人、特斯拉及xAI创始人埃隆·马斯克正式对簿公堂。这场审判的核心争议在于，马斯克指控奥特曼和OpenAI背离了公司创立时的非营利使命——开发“造福人类”的人工智能，转而以营利为目的，尤其是通过其明星产品ChatGPT追逐商业利益。马斯克本人、他的家族办公室负责人贾里德·伯查尔以及OpenAI另一位联合创始人格雷格·布罗克曼已经出庭作证。前OpenAI董事会成员、与马斯克共同育有四个孩子的希冯·齐里斯于周三（5月7日）出庭，法庭还播放了前OpenAI首席技术官米拉·穆拉蒂的视频证词。微软CEO萨提亚·纳德拉将于5月11日下周一出庭，其后OpenAI联合创始人、前首席科学家伊利亚·苏茨克维也将作证。

马斯克是OpenAI的原始联合创始人之一，他在2015年共同出资创立了这家公司。他认为，奥特曼和布罗克曼通过承诺坚持非营利、开源、造福人类的愿景，诱使他投入资金和资源；但后来OpenAI转向了商业化和闭源模式，特别是与微软建立了深度合作关系，这违背了创始协议。马斯克声称自己被“欺骗”了。奥特曼和OpenAI一方则坚决否认这一指控，称该诉讼“毫无根据，是出于嫉妒而试图阻挠竞争对手的恶意之举”，其真实目的是帮助马斯克自己的公司（SpaceX、xAI以及X平台）——xAI已经推出了ChatGPT的竞品Grok。OpenAI在庭审文件中强调，公司始终在调整使命的具体实现方式，但核心目标未变，而且马斯克本人曾支持过营利化转型。

在诉讼请求中，马斯克要求法院移除奥特曼和布罗克曼在OpenAI的领导职位，并禁止OpenAI继续以“公益公司”（public benefit corporation）的架构运营——他认为这种架构掩盖了其营利本质，违反了非营利宗旨。此外，如果马斯克胜诉，他要求OpenAI的非营利实体获得高达1500亿美元的损害赔偿（这是他索赔金额的上限）。

从各方角色来看，本案的关键人物包括：原告方——马斯克、其首席律师史蒂文·莫洛、家族办公室负责人伯查尔、前董事会成员齐里斯；被告方——奥特曼、其首席律师威廉·萨维特、OpenAI总裁布罗克曼、前首席科学家苏茨克维；主审法官是伊冯·冈萨雷斯·罗杰斯（YGR）。微软CEO纳德拉的证词将至关重要，因为微软是OpenAI的最大投资者和商业合作伙伴，其表态将直接影响法庭对OpenAI“是否背离非营利初心”的判断。

这场审判的最终结果将直接决定OpenAI的公司治理结构和未来发展路径。如果马斯克胜诉，OpenAI可能被迫剥离营利部门、重建非营利董事会，甚至终止与微软的部分合作；如果奥特曼胜诉，OpenAI将继续以现有模式运营，但公众对AI安全与商业利益之间平衡的质疑会更加强烈。无论结果如何，这一案件都会成为人工智能行业监管与伦理讨论的标志性事件。

history.title: Lian Li’s new DK07 Wood motorized standing desk doubles as a dual-PC chassis with support for E-ATX systems and massive cooling setups — five new wood-finished models are available now
history.description: The updated version adds a dark walnut finish while retaining support for dual systems, large GPUs, and extensive cooling setups.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter PC case and accessories manufacturer Lian Li has announced a new variant for its DK07 motorized standing desk. The company has retained the original launch price of $1,399.99, and the desk is available starting today through Amazon, Newegg, Caseking, Overclockers UK, and SCAN.Originally launched back in 2024, the updated version now comes in a dark walnut wood finish with a tempered glass panel and enough internal space to accommodate single or dual E-ATX systems.Check out the Lian Li DK07 Wood on AmazonCheck out the Lian Li DK07 Wood on NeweggCheck out the Lian Li DK07 Wood on CasekingCheck out the Lian Li DK07 Wood on Overclockers UKCheck out the Lian Li DK07 Wood on SCANApart from the new tabletop finish, the new DK07 Wood offers features similar to the original, including an adjustable height ranging from 676 millimeters (26.61 inches) to 1,162 millimeters (45.7 inches), along with three customizable preset positions. For accessibility, the desk comes with a sliding front panel to lift the tempered glass top when accessing internal components. Additionally, the removable modular motherboard trays make the building process more convenient, allowing users to install and prepare components outside the desk before placing the system into the chassis.Image 1 of 12(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)(Image credit: Lian Li)The desk supports both horizontal and vertical GPU orientations, with clearance of up to 383mm in length. In terms of power supply, one can install up to two full-size 220mm PSUs with up to 10x 3.5” HDD and six 2.5” SSDs. There’s plenty of cooling options as well, including support for a 480mm radiator or up to four 360mm radiators and 17 x 120mm fans in a single system setup, or up to two 360mm radiators and 12 x 120mm fans in a dual system setup. You also get dedicated front panel I/O for each system, which includes power and reset buttons, two USB 3.1 Type-C ports, two USB 3.0 Type-A, and a mic/audio jack. Other notable features include a built-in wireless charging pad, two drawers, internal cable organizers, dual cable pass-through holes for peripherals, and a rear cable tray.Latest Videos FromLian Li’s DK07 standing desk seems quite the practical solution as it not only saves on space but also doubles as a chassis for two PCs with all the proper organization one could ask for, and even a tempered glass panel to show off your premium hardware. In more than one way, it makes more sense than it initially sounds, especially at $1,399.99. Considering the amount of functionality packed into the desk, the pricing feels somewhat reasonable when compared to buying a premium standing desk and a pair of enthusiast-grade PC cases separately. That said, it remains a niche product aimed at enthusiasts.Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds. history.titleZh: 联力新款DK07木纹电动升降桌，可兼作双PC机箱，支持E-ATX系统及大型散热配置——现有五款全新木纹版本可供选择
history.descriptionZh:
PC机箱和配件制造商联力（Lian Li）为其DK07电动升降桌推出了全新变体版本——DK07 Wood。该产品保留了原始1399.99美元（约合人民币1万元）的定价，即日起通过亚马逊、新蛋、Caseking、Overclockers UK和SCAN等渠道开售。DK07最初于2024年发布，此次更新的核心变化在于桌面采用深色胡桃木饰面，并配备钢化玻璃顶板，内部空间足以容纳单路或双路E-ATX系统。

技术层面，DK07 Wood延续了原版的核心设计理念：将电动升降桌与高端PC机箱合二为一。其高度调节范围为676毫米至1162毫米（约26.6至45.7英寸），支持三个自定义预设位置的记忆存储。为方便硬件安装与维护，桌子配备可滑动的前面板，用于升起钢化玻璃顶盖；可拆卸的模块化主板托盘让用户能在桌外完成组件预装，随后整体放入桌内，显著简化了装机流程。

性能参数方面，DK07 Wood支持水平和垂直两种GPU安装方向，显卡限长383毫米。电源部分可容纳最多两个全尺寸220毫米电源。存储方面，最多可安装10个3.5英寸机械硬盘和6个2.5英寸固态硬盘。散热扩展能力是最大亮点：单系统配置下，可支持一块480毫米水冷排或最多四块360毫米水冷排，并容纳17个120毫米风扇；双系统配置下，则可支持最多两块360毫米水冷排和12个120毫米风扇。每个系统均配有独立的前面板I/O接口，包括电源和重启按钮、两个USB 3.1 Type-C端口、两个USB 3.0 Type-A端口以及一组麦克风/音频插孔。此外，桌子还内置了无线充电板、两个抽屉、内部线缆管理器、用于外设的双穿线孔以及后部线缆托盘，细节设计相当周全。

从应用场景来看，联力DK07 Wood主打节省空间与高度整合，尤其适合需要同时运行两台高性能PC的发烧友或内容创作者。它既是一张兼顾健康办公的升降桌，又是一个带有钢化玻璃侧透、可展示硬件的双系统机箱。相较于单独购买一张优质电动升降桌再加两个发烧级机箱的总成本，1399.99美元的定价显得较为合理。不过，这种将电脑桌与机箱深度绑定的设计仍属小众产品，主要面向对桌面整洁度和双系统布线有极致追求的硬件爱好者。

history.title: Motherboard sales 'collapse' by more than 25% as chipmakers strangle enthusiast PC market to build more AI chips — Asus projected to sell 5 million fewer boards in 2025, Gigabyte, MSI, and ASRock also expected to see reduced sales numbers
history.description: The combined sales of the four biggest motherboard manufacturers are expected to fall by at least 28% in 2026, with the entire industry selling 11.7 million less units due to increasing PC component costs.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 14 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Motherboard sales are now collapsing amid unprecedented shortages fueled by AI, causing prices for many major PC components to rise across the board during the past six months, with memory modules and storage drives leading the way. Now those shortages are being exacerbated by chipmakers, like Nvidia, Intel, and AMD, reducing production of consumer chips so they can manufacture more AI processors. The AI infrastructure buildout is also causing shortages for Intel and AMD CPUs (and even high-end Macs), especially as interest in agentic AI is going through the roof.Because of this, users who don’t have deep pockets are putting off upgrading their PCs and holding on to their current devices longer. Motherboard manufacturers have begun to feel the effects of these delayed purchases, with Digitimes [machine translated] reporting that the four major firms are revising their target sales downward.Asus, which sold 15 million motherboards in 2025, has only shipped a little more than 5 million in the first half of 2026. It’s expected that the company will have to push hard for it to even move 10 million units by the end of the year, marking a 33% decrease in sales year-on-year. Gigabyte and MSI sold 11.5 million and 11 million motherboards last year, respectively. However, both companies have revised their internal forecasts for 2026 to 9 million (Gigabyte) and 8.4 million (MSI), a 22% drop for the former and a 24% contraction for the latter. You may like PC makers face shortages of Intel and AMD CPUs that stretch up to six months Brace for a barren landscape of new hardware launches, as AI demand reshapes the world of consumer electronics Entry-level PC market to ‘disappear’ by 2028 Latest Videos FromASRock will be hardest hit by the situation, with the company’s shipments projected to fall by 37%, from 4.3 million in 2025 to just 2.7 million by the end of the year. This marks a contraction of 28% for the overall motherboard market, at least for the big four manufacturers.AI’s demand for memory, storage, and processors is the primary driver for this drop in sales. Shortages that have been caused by their massive purchases have forced PC builders and enthusiasts to fight over a smaller pie of PC components, resulting in higher overall prices for these components.Aside from this, AMD continues to use the AM5 socket for its latest processors, while Intel's Nova Lake, which will reportedly use LGA 1954, isn’t available until later this year. The situation is further compounded by Nvidia not releasing a refreshed RTX 50 Super series this year, while rumors claim that the RTX 60 series will not debut until 2028. This confluence of factors is discouraging PC builders from upgrading their current systems.Despite this drop in sales, these companies aren’t exactly struggling. Asus, Gigabyte, and ASRock have pivoted some of their production towards AI servers, allowing them to capture some of the investments that hyperscalers are generously pouring into their data centers. But if you’re planning to build a completely new PC from scratch, you might be able to find good deals on motherboard combos, especially as retailers are keen on getting their inventories moving. Although these discounts might not be enough to offset the increased costs of memory, storage, and, to some extent, processors, it will at least save you a few dollars as you navigate the current chip crisis.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

history.titleZh: 主板销量“暴跌”超过25%，芯片制造商为生产更多AI芯片而牺牲发烧友PC市场——华硕预计2025年减少500万块主板出货量，技嘉、微星及华擎预计也将出现销量下滑
history.descriptionZh:
人工智能基础设施的疯狂扩张正在深刻重塑消费电子市场，一场由AI芯片需求引发的连锁短缺危机已波及PC主板行业。过去六个月里，内存模块和存储驱动器价格全面上涨，而NVIDIA、英特尔和AMD等芯片制造商为了生产更多AI处理器，大幅削减了消费级芯片的产量，进一步加剧了短缺。与此同时，对Agentic AI（自主智能体）的兴趣激增，导致英特尔和AMD的CPU（甚至高端Mac）也面临供应紧张，普通用户被迫推迟更换PC，长期持有现有设备。

主板制造商首当其冲。据Digitimes报道，四大主板厂商纷纷下调销售目标：华硕在2025年售出1500万块主板，但2026年上半年仅出货500多万块，预计全年勉强达到1000万块，同比下滑33%；技嘉去年售出1150万块，今年内部目标下调至900万块（跌22%）；微星从1100万块降至840万块（跌24%）；而华擎受影响最严重，预计从430万块骤降至270万块，降幅达37%。这意味着四大厂商合计主板市场将收缩约28%。

销量暴跌的核心原因是AI对内存、存储和处理器的巨大需求。大规模采购造成的短缺迫使PC组装者和发烧友在更小的组件蛋糕中争夺，推高了整体价格。此外，技术层面也缺乏升级动力：AMD继续使用AM5插槽，英特尔即将发布的Nova Lake（采用LGA 1954）要等到今年晚些时候，而NVIDIA今年不推出RTX 50 Super系列，甚至RTX 60系列据传要到2028年才会登场——多重因素叠加让用户升级意愿降至冰点。

不过，这些厂商并未陷入困境。华硕、技嘉和华擎已将部分生产线转向AI服务器，成功捕获了超大规模云厂商为数据中心投入的巨额投资。对于想从零组装新PC的用户，目前主板套装常能遇到折扣——零售商急于清库存——虽然这些优惠可能不足以抵消内存、存储和处理器的涨价，但至少能在芯片危机中节省一些成本。消费者需要更理性地权衡升级时机，等待更好的购买窗口。

history.title: Musk's SpaceX has rented out access to its supercomputer's 220,000 Nvidia GPUs and 300 megawatts of AI compute power to rival Anthropic — Musk says “No one set off my evil detector,” Antrhropic also interested in orbital data centers
history.description: Anthropic has signed a deal with SpaceX to access the latter's massive data center, which has over 220,000 Nvidia GPUs and 300 megawatts of computing power.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Anthropic announced in a press release on Wednesday that it has signed a deal with Elon Musk’s SpaceX to use the company's massive Colossus 1 data center. According to a corresponding announcement from SpaceXAI, the deal will give Anthropic access to all of the massive supercomputer. That's over 222,000 Nvidia GPUs — including powerful H100 and H200 chips alongside next-generation GB200 accelerator systems — and 300 megawatts plus of compute power.Anthropic says this additional capacity will go toward improving the experience for paid Claude users — Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers — via three key changes. Effective yesterday, Claude Code’s five-hour rate limits have doubled for all paid tiers. The company has also removed the peak hours limit reduction for Pro and Max. Lastly, it has “considerably” raised the API rate limits, the volume of requests developers can make, for Claude Opus models.The announcement highlighted other similar agreements with Amazon, Google, and Microsoft, all aimed at building gigawatts of additional capacity. Anthropic also expressed interest in partnering with SpaceX to “multiple gigawatts of orbital AI compute capacity, basically data centers in space, as, according to SpaceX, “The compute required to train and operate the next generation of these systems is outpacing what terrestrial power, land, and cooling can deliver on the timelines that matter.” You may like Sam Altman fires back at Elon Musk's proposal for space-based data centers, says orbiting data centers 'ridiculous' for now Anthropic promises to pay for electricity price increases due to it's AI data centers OpenAI has effectively abandoned first-party Stargate data centers in favor of more flexible deals Latest Videos FromThe Colossus 1 deal means that the whole first-generation cluster, originally built to power xAI’s own Grok models, is now powering one of its direct AI rivals, as the company focuses on building Colossus 2. In an X post, Musk said he gave the green light to lease Colossus 1 to Anthropic after spending time with senior members of the company to “understand what they do to ensure Claude is good for humanity.” He claimed he “was impressed,” saying, “No one set off my evil detector.” The statement is a stark reversal of comments Musk made earlier this year about Anthropic, when he called Claude “misanthropic and evil."In the press release, Anthropic also signaled that its compute expansion strategy extends far beyond raw processing power. The company said future infrastructure deployments will increasingly target international regions, such as Europe and Asia, to meet growing enterprise demand for local data residency and regulatory compliance, particularly in sectors like healthcare, finance, and government. It also stressed that future capacity partnerships will prioritize politically stable, democratic countries with secure AI supply chains, while exploring ways to offset increases in electricity costs and reinvest in communities hosting the data centers.In addition to the compute deal announcement, Anthropic also unveiled on Wednesday a new Claude feature called 'dreaming,' designed to help its AI agents improve themselves between sessions. The feature allows Claude-powered agents to review previous work, identify recurring patterns and mistakes, and reorganize memory files that contain user preferences and contextual information, enabling the system to refine its behavior over time rather than starting each session from scratch.
